foods.name: Whey protein — Dymatize ISO100
foods.note: Hydrolyzed whey isolate, vanilla — from MesProtéine

foods.name: Chicken breast (raw, skinless)
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — was previously listed with cooked (roasted) values; raw skinless breast is ~120 kcal/100g, not 165

foods.name: Chicken breast (cooked, roasted, skinless)
foods.note: Standard USDA roasted skinless breast — weigh after cooking, not before

foods.name: Chicken wings (raw, bone-in, skin removed)
foods.note: As weighed before any prep

foods.name: Chicken drumstick (raw, bone-in, skin removed)
foods.note: As weighed before any prep

foods.name: Chicken full leg (raw, bone-in, skin removed)
foods.note: As weighed before any prep

foods.name: Kefta 80/20 (beef/veal mix, raw)
foods.note: ~20% fat raw mince, standard Moroccan kefta — corrected 2026-07, fat grams now match the 20% label (was inconsistently listed at 15g/100g)

foods.name: Kefta 80/20 (beef/veal mix, cooked/grilled)
foods.note: ~20% fat, weighed after grilling — moisture loss concentrates protein/minerals, some fat renders out in drippings

foods.name: Kefta 90/10 (beef/veal mix, raw)
foods.note: ~10% fat raw mince, lean Moroccan kefta

foods.name: Kefta 90/10 (beef/veal mix, cooked/grilled)
foods.note: ~10% fat, weighed after grilling — moisture loss concentrates protein/minerals more than the fat, which partly renders out

foods.name: Bœuf (ground beef, 90/10, raw)
foods.note: 90% lean / 10% fat raw mince

foods.name: Sheep (lamb, raw, lean+fat)
foods.note: Leg, trimmed to 1/4" fat

foods.name: Thon Isabel (en aceite vegetal)
foods.note: In vegetable oil, 3×80g pack

foods.name: Egg (average size)
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — Mehdi weighed his actual rumi (regular) eggs at ~65g raw with shell; shell is ~11% of weight, so edible portion is ~58g. Values rescaled from the USDA per-100g density (previously mislabeled at ~44g)
foods.serving: per egg (~58g edible, ~65g raw with shell)

foods.name: Egg (Beldi, free-range)
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — Mehdi weighed his actual beldi eggs at ~48g raw with shell; shell is ~11% of weight, so edible portion is ~43g. Values rescaled from the previously-implied per-100g density (was mislabeled at ~44g, same as rumi, which understated the real size gap). Beldi stays richer per gram (more protein/fat density, deeper yolk) but is a physically smaller egg than rumi
foods.serving: per egg (~43g edible, ~48g raw with shell)

foods.name: Egg white (raw)
foods.note: Standard USDA raw egg white values — used when a recipe separates whites from yolks (e.g. the standard menu breakfast)

foods.name: Egg yolk (raw)
foods.note: Standard USDA raw egg yolk values — used when a recipe separates whites from yolks (e.g. the standard menu breakfast)

foods.name: Barley — ch3ir (pearled, dry, raw)
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — fiber was the hulled-barley figure; pearled barley (bran removed) is lower

foods.name: Pumpkin seeds (hulled kernels, raw)
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — macros previously didn't match the Zinc/Magnesium values (those were already sourced from hulled kernel data); now consistent throughout

foods.name: Black olives (oil-cured / dry-cured)
foods.note: Moroccan zeitoun beldi style, sold loose by weight at market — no label, generic estimate; very high sodium

foods.name: Pumpkin
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — kcal/protein/carbs were under USDA raw pumpkin values

foods.name: Garlic
foods.note: Corrected 2026-07 — carbs/kcal were under the standard USDA raw garlic value

foods.name: Lettuce (iceberg, raw)
foods.note: Standard USDA raw iceberg lettuce values

foods.name: Dates
foods.note: Deglet Noor type

foods.name: Peach
foods.note: Standard USDA raw peach values

foods.name: Blueberries (Fresh)
foods.note: Raw fresh blueberries — USDA standard values

foods.name: Sugar (white, granulated)
foods.note: 1 tsp ≈ 4g

foods.name: Mustard (prepared, yellow)
foods.note: Standard USDA prepared mustard values. 1 tsp ≈ 5g

foods.name: Mayonnaise (homemade, boiled egg base)
foods.note: 1 whole boiled beldi egg + 1 tsp mustard + 1 tsp lemon juice + pinch salt + 40g olive oil — chosen over a raw-egg/100g-oil version for less fat per 100g against the protocol's fat target

foods.name: Mayonnaise (homemade, raw egg base)
foods.note: 1 whole raw beldi egg + 1 tsp mustard + 1 tsp lemon juice + pinch salt + 100g olive oil — the richer, classic-texture version; carries raw-egg food-safety considerations

foods.name: Black coffee
foods.note: Brewed, no sugar. 1 cup ≈ 240ml

foods.name: Mint tea (herbal, no sugar)
foods.note: Brewed fresh mint in water. 1 glass ≈ 200ml

foods.name: White rice — Cigala (dry, raw)
foods.note: Long grain white rice, Cigala brand — values from USDA long grain white rice

foods.name: Brown rice — Cigala (dry, raw)
foods.note: Whole grain brown rice, Cigala brand — values from USDA brown rice

foods.name: Jaouda Perly Nature (fromage frais)
foods.note: Plain fresh cheese — Jaouda brand, 1 cup = 85g (barcode 6111242100985)

foods.name: Jaouda Lait UHT Entier Sans Lactose (lactose-free whole milk)
foods.note: Whole UHT milk with lactose pre-broken down by lactase — ~0.1g residual lactose/100ml, functionally lactose-free. Also the base for homemade raib/lben.

foods.name: Raib (homemade, from Jaouda Sans Lactose)
foods.note: Fermented whole milk, cultured from Jaouda Lait Sans Lactose — fermentation barely changes the base milk's macros (comparable to plain whole-milk yogurt), and consumes what little lactose remained

foods.name: Lben (homemade, churned from Raib)
foods.note: Buttermilk left after churning raib to separate the butterfat (smen) — thinner and much lower fat than raib, protein/calcium mostly stay in the liquid

foods.name: Eau Sidi Ali (natural mineral water)
foods.note: #1 best Moroccan water — only natural spring source of the 4; balanced minerals, pH 7.5, very low chlorides

foods.name: Eau Ciel (table water, Coca-Cola)
foods.note: #2 — richest in Ca & Mg of the 4, high bicarbonates; table water with notably high chlorides (130mg/L)

foods.name: Eau Ghayt (table water)
foods.note: #3 — table water with modest mineralization; exact label values not publicly available online

foods.name: Aquafina (purified water, PepsiCo)
foods.note: #4 worst — reverse osmosis strips all minerals (TDS ~4mg/L); safe but avoid as your primary daily water
